current_pack.override_reason: Generated C pack has 28 units outside 40-150 because ledger-order cohesion is smaller than the default risk range.

canonical_identity.vault_policy: Vault stores Matter app matter_code as projection/reference and does not create a separate namespace

sensitive_service_precheck_boundary.risk_a_pack_reason: permission/audit/tenant/idempotency/lock/persistence sensitive boundary remains small at 10 units

permission_audit_tail_fixture.risk_a_pack_reason: permission/audit binding tail plus base tenant fixture touches tenant, permission, audit, idempotency, replay, no-real-data, and fixture boundaries, so it remains a 10-unit Risk A pack

synthetic_fixture_permission_substrate.risk_c_pack_reason: fixture, evidence, review packet, handoff, and P06 permission substrate rows are grouped as one large generated Risk C pack while keeping runtime permission/audit behavior closed

permission_substrate_workflow_binding.risk_b_pack_reason: CP00-187 binds secondary workflow and permission/audit descriptor rows across 40 units while preserving no runtime permission evaluation, no audit write, no route dispatch, and no live UI rendering

permission_audit_security_fixture_bounda.risk_a_pack_reason: CP00-188 covers security trimming, audit expectation, permission fixture, allowed/denied/cross-tenant/leak tests, and the M06 matrix/view/search entry; it stays small because these rows touch permission, audit, tenant, leak, and fixture boundaries

synthetic_fixture_failure_evidence_conti.risk_c_pack_reason: CP00-189 groups repeated synthetic fixture, evidence/review/closeout, and failure taxonomy descriptors; each row stays synthetic, no-write, customer-safe, and non-executing

failure_receipt_taxonomy_boundary.risk_a_pack_reason: CP00-190 keeps blocked-claim receipts, failure fixture evidence, audit failure hints, Hermes failure evidence, edge-case Claude prompt, human escalation note, failure taxonomy, and missing-tenant failure small because they touch permission/audit/tenant/failure boundary semantics

generated_failure_recovery_binding.risk_b_pack_reason: CP00-191 groups generated failure recovery and permission/audit binding rows because the pack is a source-backed descriptor workflow boundary, while permission evaluation, audit writes, rollback, retry, route dispatch, API handlers, Citation Ledger, and Loop execution stay disabled.

failure_fixture_entry_boundary.risk_a_pack_reason: CP00-192 keeps the Claude/human tail and initial M06 failure fixture rows small because they touch review escalation, tenant, actor, matter, resource, cross-tenant, and permission-denied failure boundaries.

failure_fixture_evidence_review_bridge.risk_c_pack_reason: CP00-193 groups repeated failure fixture, golden test, Hermes evidence, Claude review, closeout handoff, and RP05.P08 evidence/semantics rows as a Risk C descriptor catalog. Runtime permission evaluation, audit writes, recovery execution, API handlers, live DOM, Citation Ledger, and Loop remain disabled.

permission_audit_evidence_terminal_bridg.risk_b_pack_reason: CP00-194 continues the RP05.P08 evidence/semantic bridge through permission/audit binding and synthetic fixture evidence rows. It is grouped as Risk B because it binds workflow semantics, validation command checks, blocked/review/approval markers, and handoff readiness without executing runtime permission, audit, recovery, API, DOM, Citation Ledger, or Loop behavior.

evidence_review_handoff_terminal_bridge.risk_c_pack_reason: CP00-195 closes the RP05.P08 evidence/review/handoff terminal rows and opens RP05.P09 review-question planning rows as one Risk C pack. It records coverage, review prompts, security/audit questions, UI leak questions, downstream readiness, risk routing, and handoff semantics without implementing runtime permission, audit, API, DOM, Citation Ledger, or Loop behavior.

review_question_security_gate.risk_a_pack_reason: CP00-196 isolates the RP05.P09.M07.S03-S12 security/audit review questions as a Risk A gate. It records permission-bypass, audit-completeness, missing-test, UI-leak, risk, severity, go/no-go, finding-routing, and human-approval semantics without evaluating runtime permissions, writing audit events, dispatching routes, rendering live DOM, or implementing Citation Ledger or Loop behavior.
